# hrobot_storagebox_subaccount (Resource)

Manages a sub-account on a Hetzner Storage Box. The Storage Box itself is ordered outside Terraform and referenced by `storagebox_id`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# The Storage Box is ordered outside Terraform; reference it by ID.
resource "hrobot_storagebox_subaccount" "backup" {
  storagebox_id  = 12345
  home_directory = "/backups"
  samba          = true
  ssh            = true
}

# The auto-generated password is available (sensitive) for the initial setup.
output "subaccount_password" {
  value     = hrobot_storagebox_subaccount.backup.password
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `home_directory` (String) Home directory of the sub-account, relative to the Storage Box root.
- `storagebox_id` (Number) ID of the Storage Box that owns the sub-account. Changing it replaces the resource.

### Optional

- `comment` (String) Free-form comment.
- `external_reachability` (Boolean) Allow access from outside the Hetzner network.
- `readonly` (Boolean) Make the sub-account read-only.
- `samba` (Boolean) Enable Samba/CIFS access.
- `ssh` (Boolean) Enable SSH access.
- `webdav` (Boolean) Enable WebDAV access.

### Read-Only

- `account_id` (String) Sub-account ID.
- `create_time` (String) Creation timestamp.
- `id` (String) Composite identifier `<storagebox_id>/<username>`.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Auto-generated password, returned only at creation.
- `server` (String) Hostname clients connect to.
- `username` (String) Sub-account username, assigned by Hetzner.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Sub-accounts are imported as <storagebox_id>/<username>.
terraform import hrobot_storagebox_subaccount.backup 12345/u12345-sub1
```
